warmup.name: Neck Rolls
warmup.instruction: Slowly roll your head in a full circle — 5 times clockwise, 5 times counter-clockwise. Keep shoulders relaxed.

warmup.name: Arm Circles
warmup.instruction: Extend arms wide. Make large circles forward for 15 seconds, then backward for 15 seconds.

warmup.name: Hip Circles
warmup.instruction: Hands on hips, feet shoulder-width apart. Rotate your hips in large circles — 10 each direction.

warmup.name: Leg Swings
warmup.instruction: Hold a wall for balance. Swing one leg forward and backward like a pendulum. Keep it controlled, not forced.

warmup.name: Standing Quad Stretch
warmup.instruction: Stand on one leg, bend the other knee and hold your ankle behind you. Keep hips forward and knees together.

warmup.name: Calf Raises
warmup.instruction: Stand on the edge of a step or flat ground. Rise up on your toes slowly, then lower. Builds calf strength for pedaling.

warmup.name: Easy 5-Minute Spin
warmup.instruction: Get on your bike in your easiest gear. Spin lightly at very low effort for 5 full minutes to warm up your muscles and joints.

cooldown.name: 2-Minute Easy Spin
cooldown.instruction: Keep pedaling in your easiest gear at very low effort. This flushes lactic acid from your muscles and brings your heart rate down.

cooldown.name: Standing Hamstring Stretch
cooldown.instruction: Stand, extend one leg forward with heel on ground and toes up. Hinge forward at the hips (keep back flat) until you feel the stretch in the back of the thigh.

cooldown.name: Figure Four Hip Stretch
cooldown.instruction: Sit or stand. Cross one ankle over the opposite knee forming a figure-4 shape. Press down gently on the raised knee. Cyclists need this one — long saddle time tightens the glutes.

cooldown.name: Chest Opener
cooldown.instruction: Interlace fingers behind your back. Gently squeeze shoulder blades together and lift your hands slightly. Counter the hunched riding position.

cooldown.name: Cat-Cow Back Stretch
cooldown.instruction: On all fours: arch your back up (cat) then drop your belly down (cow). Alternate slowly. Essential for the lower back strain from cycling posture.

cooldown.name: Wall Calf Stretch
cooldown.instruction: Stand facing a wall, one foot back with heel flat on the ground. Lean into the wall until you feel the calf stretch. Switch legs.

cooldown.name: Deep Breathing (4-7-8)
cooldown.instruction: Inhale for 4 counts, hold for 7, exhale slowly for 8. This activates the parasympathetic nervous system — bringing you from 'fight or flight' to full recovery mode.
